description:
Symptom: every release-plz PR (#1-#15 historical, #30 current) was pulled into balls as a task. Closing such a task triggers push.rs::patch_issue, which not only fails to actually close the PR (state=closed via issues API is a noop on a merged PR) but also rewrites the PR title to append [bl-id]. Observed during the bl-094b dogfood: closing bl-0eef edited PR #15's title from 'chore: release v0.4.0' to 'chore: release v0.4.0 [bl-0eef]', polluting the public release history. Reverted by hand via 'gh issue edit 15 --title ...'.

ROOT CAUSE
GitHub's REST endpoint GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues returns BOTH issues and pull requests. Each response object carries a 'pull_request' sub-object only when the entry is a PR. The github-issues plugin's pull (balls-github-plugin/crates/balls-plugin-github-issues/src/pull.rs) iterates the response without filtering on that field, so every release-plz PR becomes a SyncCreate.

CASCADES
- 7 stuck 'born-closed' balls tasks today (bl-7734, bl-2ad3, bl-8f47, bl-e6a9, bl-76bc, bl-8120, bl-3c3d for PRs #14, #13, #12, #11, #10, #9, #8). These can't be cleaned until either this lands or bl-321d (born-closed close-mirror) lands.
- Closing any PR-backed balls task via balls rewrites the PR title on GH (bad).
- Each future release-plz cycle would otherwise add a fresh stuck task on every minor/patch.

FIX
In pull.rs (or whichever module enumerates issues from the GH API response): skip every entry where the response object's 'pull_request' field is present (it's an Option<PullRequestRef> in serde — Some => skip, None => issue). One-line guard; the existing test scaffolding under pull_emit_tests.rs already exercises classify and SyncCreate emission, so the regression test is straightforward.

Out of scope: there is a legitimate use case for mirroring PRs into balls (the github plugin already does that for deferred-mode review tasks). But that lives in balls-plugin-github::sync, NOT in github-issues. The two plugins are independent participants and should not be confused.

REGRESSION TEST
Feed pull a SearchResultV3 response containing one issue and one PR. Assert the resulting SyncReport has exactly one Created/Known, and the PR is silently dropped.

CLEANUP
After this lands and a sync runs:
  1. Existing 7 PR-backed balls tasks become orphaned (no GH counterpart per the new filter). Either:
     a. plugin emits SyncDelete for entries no longer present in the GH view, balls archives them, OR
     b. user archives manually with 'bl update <id> status=closed --note "PR; no longer pulled"' + disable plugin briefly to avoid the title-rewrite side effect.
  2. v0.5.0 release PR will no longer appear in bl list (bl-fa2a is currently the canonical for #30 PR — that task should also archive).
title: github-issues plugin ingests PRs as issues (no pull_request filter)
